key_departures.impact: Traded for a 2026 fifth-round pick. Howard was Houston's best offensive lineman in 2025, starting games at RT, RG, and LG with a career-low 3.4% pressure rate allowed. His departure removes the team's most versatile and reliable OL piece for just a fifth-round return, escalating an already critical OL need. This is the second straight year HOU traded away its best lineman (Tunsil in 2025).

Traded to Detroit as part of the David Montgomery package (with a 2026 4th-round and 2027 7th-round pick). Scruggs started just 1 game in 2025 after starting 13 in 2024. Loss of IOL depth, especially at center where he had logged snaps. Escalates center need.

Released at his own request before the start of the 2026 league year. Mixon missed all of 2025 with a foot injury. The release cleared $8M in cap space. Was not a functional contributor anyway; offset by the David Montgomery trade.
Signed a 3-year, $25.5M deal with Washington. Settle was a starting DT alongside Rankins. His departure creates a need at DT depth behind Rankins and Tommy Togiai.
Signed with Atlanta. Harris had a history of injuries and was largely displaced by E.J. Speed in 2025. Depth loss only — E.J. Speed re-signing mitigates this entirely.
Was not expected to return per multiple reports. Slot receiver depth loss, but Nico Collins and Tank Dell return. Minimal impact on WR need picture.
Not expected to return; mulling retirement at 35. Had been a rotational EDGE piece. Departure opens a depth spot behind Hunter and Anderson, partially addressed by Logan Hall signing.
Not expected to return. Was a depth/veteran presence; Montgomery trade and Woody Marks' return make this a non-issue.
UFA, not re-signed. Was rotational EDGE depth. Departure adds to EDGE depth need alongside Autry's exit.
Not expected to return due to durability issues. Was IDL depth behind Rankins and Settle. Minimal impact given Tommy Togiai remains and Logan Hall can play inside.
UFA punter, replaced by Kai Kroeger trade from New Orleans. Net-neutral at the position.

signings.analysis: One-year, $40.1M extension fully guaranteed, agreed March 5-6. Hunter posted 15 sacks and second-team All-Pro honors in 2025. This is his second straight one-year extension in Houston; it keeps him through the 2027 season. At $40.1M AAV he is the 4th-highest paid pass rusher in the NFL. With Will Anderson Jr. also locked up on his existing deal, EDGE is fully addressed at the starter level. Only rotational/depth EDGE remains a need given Autry and Barnett departures.

Three-year, $37.5M deal with $20M fully guaranteed at signing and $23.5M total guaranteed. Agreed March 9 before tampering window opened. Ingram was PFF top-10 run-blocking guard in 2025 and allowed the 12th-lowest pressure rate among guards. Re-signing the RG anchor is a major win. However, with Juice Scruggs traded and no center upgrade signed, the center position (Jake Andrews as starter) remains a glaring open need. IOL partially addressed on the guard side but critical center need remains.

Three-year, $24.75M deal with $16.5M guaranteed. Signed March 9 (officially March 13). Blankenship is a proven starting safety with 308 career tackles and nine interceptions over four Eagles seasons; was team captain in 2025. He joins Calen Bullock in a starting safety tandem. At $8.25M AAV on a 3-year deal with meaningful guarantees, this is a real starter-level investment. S need fully addressed — secondary is now elite with Stingley, Lassiter, Pitre, Bullock, and Blankenship.

Two-year, $25M deal with $13.5M fully guaranteed and $8.5M signing bonus. Signed March 11-13. Smith was a starter for 8 seasons with the Colts, playing 107 games with 105 starts, mostly at RT. He started 13 games in 2025 before a concussion/neck injury, posting a 51st percentile pass-block win rate — better than what Tytus Howard produced. At $12.5M AAV with only 1 year of real guaranteed money, this is a bridge/prove-it starter deal. OT partially addressed: Smith is the likely RT starter, Aireontae Ersery holds LT, and Trent Brown is the swing. Still a thin unit needing draft investment.

Acquired via trade March 11 (agreed March 2). Houston sent Juice Scruggs, a 2026 4th-round pick, and a 2027 7th-round pick to Detroit. Montgomery has two years remaining on his deal at $5.49M (2026) and $7.49M (2027) with no remaining guarantees. A physical, red-zone specialist with 33 rushing TDs since 2023. He immediately becomes the RB1 alongside Woody Marks. RB need fully addressed — the Texans specifically passed on higher-cost free agent RBs (Walker, Etienne, Dowdle) in favor of this lower-cost trade. Cost: lost a 4th-round pick and IOL depth (Scruggs).

Two-year deal with $12M guaranteed (well over $6M AAV). Agreed March 9. Rankins started all 17 games in 2025 with 35 tackles, 3 sacks, and a fumble return TD in the playoff win over Pittsburgh. Re-signing him keeps one starting DT in place. However, with Tim Settle Jr. gone to Washington and Fatukasi not returning, the depth behind Rankins and Tommy Togiai is thin. IDL partially addressed at the starter tier; need remains for a DT complement or depth addition.

One-year, $12.6M extension (with total $17.6M guaranteed across 2026-27). Agreed March 7. Schultz posted a career-high 82 catches for 777 yards and 3 TDs in 2025. At $12.6M AAV he is at the top of the starter tier for TEs. Extension keeps him through 2027. With Foster Moreau added as a blocking TE2, the TE room is set. TE need fully addressed.

Two-year, $13.75M deal with $9M guaranteed ($5.75M signing bonus). Signed March 12-13 from Tampa Bay. Hall is a 2022 second-round pick with inside-outside versatility. Had 5.5 sacks in 2024 and 1.5 in 2025 in a larger role (39 tackles, 6 QB hits). Will compete as first EDGE off the bench behind Hunter and Anderson, filling the rotational role vacated by Autry. With $9M guaranteed on a 2-year deal, this is a meaningful depth/rotational investment. EDGE depth partially addressed; they still have a developmental need for the future given both Hunter (1-yr deals) and Anderson's upcoming extension.

One-year deal worth up to $4M. Robinson was a depth piece for Chicago for four seasons and will fill the same role in Houston. Minimal impact on need tier — this is camp/rotation depth, not a solution.

Two-year, $13M max deal (guaranteed ~$8M estimated). Agreed March 7. Speed logged 62 tackles and 3 TFLs in 2025, starting 9 games and earning a 92.0 PFF grade vs. Vikings. He is a physical run defender and special teams standout. With Al-Shaair and Henry To'oto'o also returning, the LB corps is deep and addressed. LB need fully addressed; Harris departure is a non-issue.

One-year deal worth up to $7M. Agreed March 6. Brown started 7 games in 2025 after a knee issue in Week 10, posting the 6th-lowest pressure rate allowed (7.1%) among RT-eligible tackles in Weeks 10-16. At 32, this is a bridge/insurance deal. With Braden Smith as the primary RT, Brown is a swing tackle and injury backstop. OT need only minimally addressed by this signing on its own — it only matters in the context of Braden Smith as the true starter.

One-year, ~$2.7M deal. Veteran depth safety retained for his special teams value and secondary versatility. Not a needle-mover at the starter level; Reed Blankenship handles the starter need. Low-cost retention of a useful roster piece.

One-year, ~$2M deal per reports. Interior DL depth re-sign. Provides rotational presence behind Rankins and Togiai. Partially offsets Tim Settle's departure at the depth level, but not a starter-caliber solution.

Two-year, $6.3M fully guaranteed deal (plus up to $1M in incentives). Signed March 11-13. Moreau is a blocking-first TE who gives the Texans a TE2 with inline blocking ability. TE was already addressed via Schultz extension. This deepens the room and provides Harrison Bryant's replacement as the blocking TE. No need impact — TE was not a pre-FA need after Schultz returned.
Two-year, $13M extension making Fairbairn the highest-paid kicker in NFL history. Agreed March 11. Led the NFL with 44 field goals made in 2025 and went 9-for-13 from 50+. K need fully locked up.

Acquired via trade with New Orleans (HOU sent a 2028 6th-round pick, received Kroeger and a 2028 7th-round pick). Kroeger replaces Tommy Townsend as the starting punter. P need addressed — minimal trade cost.
Signed March 13. Special teams/depth LB. Veteran minimum-tier signing. No meaningful need impact.

recommended_need_adjustments.reasoning: Tytus Howard (starter) traded away for a 5th-round pick, creating a genuine hole. Braden Smith signing (2yr/$25M, $13.5M gtd) partially addresses RT as a proven starter, and Trent Brown (1yr/$7M) provides swing depth. But with Aireontae Ersery still developing at LT and no proven long-term answer, OT remains a significant need for depth and a future starter. Recommend drafting an OT on Day 1 or 2.

Ed Ingram re-sign (3yr/$37.5M, $23.5M gtd) locks up RG for 3 years — a major win. But Juice Scruggs was traded (center depth lost), and Jake Andrews remains a question mark as starting center. Top centers in FA (Linderbaum, Mays) signed elsewhere. Center remains a legitimate need. Still needs a starter-quality IOL via draft or free agency late-adds. IOL drops from critical to meaningful need.

Rankins re-signed (2yr/$14M) keeps a proven starter, and Naquan Jones (1yr depth) returns. But Tim Settle Jr. signed a 3-year deal with Washington. Tommy Togiai is the DT2, which is a question mark for a full season. Logan Hall provides some interior versatility but is primarily an EDGE. IDL depth need remains at Tier 2 — need a reliable DT complement or developmental piece in the draft.

Hunter extension (1yr/$40.1M, fully gtd) + Anderson under contract = elite starting duo locked. Logan Hall (2yr/$13.75M) signed as primary depth/rotational piece, replacing Autry's role. Dominique Robinson adds low-cost depth. EDGE drops from a priority need to a developmental depth consideration — could use a Day 3 developmental rusher but no longer a premium need.

Reed Blankenship (3yr/$24.75M, $16.5M gtd) signed as starter, joining Calen Bullock. M.J. Stewart retained for depth/special teams. Secondary is now among the NFL's best with Stingley, Lassiter, Pitre, Bullock, and Blankenship. S need eliminated — drafting a safety would only be done for developmental depth.

David Montgomery trade (2yr/$12.99M remaining) gives the Texans a proven RB1 with 33 TDs since 2023. Woody Marks returns as the RB2. Joe Mixon released (was injured anyway). RB need fully resolved. No draft capital needed here.

Dalton Schultz extension (1yr/$12.6M, $17.6M total gtd) keeps the starter through 2027 after a career-high 82-catch season. Foster Moreau (2yr/$6.3M fully gtd) added as a blocking TE2. TE room is set and deep. No draft need at this position.

Azeez Al-Shaair (mutual extension interest, returning), Henry To'oto'o (returning), E.J. Speed re-signed (2yr/$13M). Three viable LBs at various levels. Christian Harris departure is a depth non-event given Speed replaced him. LB is not a draft priority.

No significant WR additions in free agency. Christian Kirk not returning. Nico Collins and Tank Dell return as WR1/WR2, but WR3 depth is thin. Houston did not address WR in free agency — it remains a Day 2-3 draft need for depth and a developmental third option.
Stingley (3yr/$90M) and Lassiter returning as one of the NFL's best CB duos. Jalen Pitre anchors the slot. No free agency activity needed or executed here. CB is not a draft need.

key_additions.headline: Second straight one-year extension locks in elite 15-sack pass rusher through the 2027 season
Top-10 PFF run-blocking guard retained as the anchor of an overhauled offensive line
Proven starting safety upgrades an already elite secondary alongside Calen Bullock
8-year Colts starter (105 career starts) takes over as the new RT anchor
33 rushing TDs since 2023 — red-zone specialist immediately becomes RB1 in Houston
Career-high 82-catch TE extended through 2027 as offensive centerpiece under C.J. Stroud
Veteran DT anchor re-signed after playoff fumble-return TD vs. Pittsburgh
2022 second-round pick arrives as primary EDGE rotation piece replacing Autry's role
NFL's highest-paid kicker locked up after leading the league with 44 field goals in 2025
Blocking-first TE2 adds inline versatility and depth behind starter Dalton Schultz

key_losses.headline: Best OL in 2025 (career-low 3.4% pressure rate) traded away for second straight offseason in a major OL overhaul
Interior OL depth traded to Lions as part of David Montgomery acquisition, deepening center uncertainty
Starting DT departs on a major deal, leaving Rankins and Togiai as the IDL tandem
Injury-plagued starter released at his own request after missing all of 2025 with a foot injury
35-year-old rotational EDGE did not return; replaced in scheme by Logan Hall signing
Slot WR depth departs, leaving Nico Collins and Tank Dell as the top two with thin WR3 depth
